elements.sent-2411-001: We prove new instances of Halin's end degree conjecture within $\mathrm{ZFC}$.
elements.sent-2411-002: In particular, we prove that there is a proper class of cardinals $\kappa$ for which Haliln's end-degree conjecture holds.
elements.sent-2411-003: This answers two questions posed by Geschke, Kurkofka, Melcher and Pitz in 2023.
elements.sent-2411-004: Furthermore, we comment on the relationship between Halin's conjecture and the Singular Cardinal Hypothesis, deriving consistency strength from failures of the former.
elements.sent-2411-005: We also show that Halin's conjecture fails on finite intervals of successors of singular cardinals in Meremovich's model.
elements.sent-2411-006: In the study of infinite graphs, the notion of ends is an endeavor to give a notion of ``direction'' to undirected graphs, by adding ``vertices at infinity''.
elements.sent-2411-007: Namely, an end of a graph is represented by rays that are connected by infinitely many vertex-disjoint paths.
elements.sent-2411-008: Continuing the work from \cite{geschke2023halin , we search for special configurations inside the ends of an underlying graph.}
elements.sent-2411-009: In particular, such configurations will contain stars of rays.

elements.sent-2411-011: In a graph $G$, a ray is a one-way infinite path, whose infinite connected subgraphs are called its tails.
elements.sent-2411-012: Say that two of its rays $r$ and $r'$ are equivalent if they are infinitely connected.
elements.sent-2411-013: The degree $\mathrm{deg (\varepsilon)$ of an end $\varepsilon$ is the maximum cardinality of a collection of pairwise disjoint rays in $\varepsilon$.}
elements.sent-2411-014: Observing that prototypes of ends of any prescribed degree are given by the Cartesian product of a sufficiently large connected graph with a ray, Halin proved his famous grid theorem (see \cite{geschke2023halin ): \begin{namedtheorem [Halin's grid] Every graph with an end of infinite degree contains a subdivision of the hexagonal quarter grid whose rays belong to that end.}}
elements.sent-2411-015: Given an end $\varepsilon$ in a graph $G$, a ray graph for $\varepsilon$ is a connected ray graph in $G$ on a degree-witnessing subset of $\varepsilon$.

elements.sent-2411-155: We are grateful to Matheus Koveroff Bellini for insightful and stimulating discussions on Halin's end degree conjecture.. \bibliographystyle{amsplain.}
elements.sent-2411-156: We prove new instances of Halin's end degree conjecture (HC) within $\mathrm{ZFC}$.
elements.sent-2411-157: In particular, we prove that there is a proper class of cardinals $\kappa$ for which Halin's end-degree conjecture holds.
elements.sent-2411-158: Furthermore, we comment on the relationship between HC and the Singular Cardinal Hypothesis, deriving consistency strength from failures of the former.
elements.sent-2411-159: We verify that Halin's conjecture fails on finite intervals of successors of singular cardinals in Meremovich's model, this is a new independence result on HC.
elements.sent-2411-160: Given an infinite graph $G=(V,E)$ a sequence $r=\{ v_n \mid n \in \omega \} \subseteq V$ is called a ray of the graph $G$ if it is a one-to-one sequence such that $\{v_n,v_{n+1}\} \in E$ for all $n \in \omega$.
